mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean forward return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of declining retail sales and rising unemployment creates a joint demand-labor contraction signal that is more predictive of negative equity returns than either series in isolation, as it captures both income and spending deterioration.
failure_condition: The interaction term adds no incremental predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) over a 5-year rolling window, or joint signals fail to produce negative returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals supply-side constraints transitioning into demand destruction and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly different from zero (p > 0.05) in late-cycle periods, or the mechanism generates positive returns in more than 50% of instances within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets revalue to higher earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns follow fewer than 55% of GDP growth instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, restrictive monetary policy raises the probability of a hard landing; sustained levels at this threshold predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as credit conditions tighten and discount rates remain elevated.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer prices and falling housing starts creates a stagflationary supply-demand imbalance that compresses corporate margins and predicts negative equity returns over 20 trading days, an effect absent when these variables move in the same direction.
failure_condition: The conditional interaction effect fails to produce negative returns in more than 60% of opposing-movement instances, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the early stage of labor market deterioration, a month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets rapidly revise growth expectations downward, an effect that diminishes once unemployment has already risen significantly.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns follow fewer than 55% of month-over-month PAYEMS declines during early deterioration phases, or the signal's hit rate is indistinguishable from random (binomial p > 0.05).
